mnemonics: PIMP CIRCLES - The OS is like a PIMP, managing its Resources (CIRCLES): CPU, I/O, Memory, Communications, Links (files), Execution, Storage.
keyPoints: An operating system acts as a resource manager, allocating CPU time, memory space, file storage, and I/O devices to processes.
Key structures for resource management include the Process Scheduler, Memory Manager, File System, and I/O System.
The goal of resource management is to provide efficient and fair access to resources, often resolving conflicts between competing processes.
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of the OS (e.g., thinking the file system manages CPU scheduling).
Assuming resource allocation is static rather than dynamic and changing as processes are created and destroyed.
